insight:
Yesterday was a solid active recovery day with 9,653 steps and no formal training sessions, which is perfect timing just 6 days out from Chester. Your body burned 2,471 calories naturally, showing your metabolism is firing well. However, that 5.8 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day - your body needs more recovery time to absorb all your hard training.

Your HRV at 95ms sits right in your optimal baseline range (93-115ms), and that low resting heart rate of 49 bpm shows excellent cardiovascular fitness. With stress levels manageable at 34/100, your body is responding well to the taper. Go ahead with today's planned session - your recovery markers are green across the board.

Focus heavily on sleep tonight - aim for 8+ hours. Your low sleep debt is the only red flag in otherwise excellent data. Consider an earlier bedtime routine and limit screens after 9pm to optimize recovery before race week intensifies.
